title: High Rise Fire Sprinkler Incentive Act of 2025
short_title: High Rise Fire Sprinkler Incentive Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
plain_summary: This bill helps building owners who add fire sprinkler systems to tall apartment buildings. It lets them save money on their taxes faster than before. Instead of taking many years to get the tax break, building owners can now get it over just fifteen years. This makes it cheaper for them to install sprinklers, which helps keep people safer in tall buildings.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the High Rise Fire Sprinkler Incentive Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Classification of Certain Automatic Fire Sprinkler System Retrofits
sections.text: This section changes the tax rules for building owners who add fire sprinkler systems to existing tall buildings. The law makes changes to the Internal Revenue Code (the main tax law for the United States). When someone installs an automatic fire sprinkler system retrofit (adding sprinklers to a building that did not have them before), they can claim the cost as a business expense over fifteen years instead of a longer period. This helps building owners save money on their taxes sooner. The law defines automatic fire sprinkler system retrofit property as sprinkler systems that meet National Fire Protection Association standards and are installed in residential buildings. The building must be one that was already built and in use before the sprinklers were added. The building must also be tall enough that it has floors more than seventy five feet above where fire trucks can reach. These new tax rules will start working after this law is passed.
